params.text:
What the provider must do

When a notifiable safety incident happens, Regulation 20 sets out clear steps the provider must take. These steps put the being-open principles into a formal process, so that the person is told the truth, supported, and kept informed as any enquiries continue.

  • Notify in person: tell the person, or their representative, as soon as reasonably practicable.
  • Explain and apologise: give a true account of what is known and offer an apology.
  • Follow up in writing: confirm the account and the results of any enquiries in writing, and keep a record.

Your part in the provider’s duty

Although these formal steps are the organisation’s legal responsibility, they can only happen if you play your part. The provider cannot notify anyone about an incident it does not know about, and cannot give a true account of facts it has not been told. Your honest, prompt report is the foundation the whole process is built on.

You may also be asked to help with the practical steps — staying with the person, offering comfort, or giving an accurate account of what you saw. Always follow your manager’s lead on who makes the formal contact and what is said, so that the person receives one clear, consistent and honest message rather than several different ones.

params.question: Under Regulation 20, how must the provider first notify the person of a notifiable safety incident?

answers.text: In person, as soon as reasonably practicable, with a true account of what is known and an apology.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: Yes. The initial notification is given in person and promptly, followed up in writing.

answers.text: Only in writing, several weeks after all enquiries are complete.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: The person is told in person and promptly; the written follow-up comes afterwards, not instead.

answers.text: By waiting until the person asks what happened.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: The duty is proactive — the provider tells the person without waiting to be asked.

answers.text: By posting a general notice for all service users to read.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: Notification is made directly and personally to the individual affected or their representative.
